source_jobs.title: Senior ML Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Founded in the US in 2022 and now based in London, UK, Recraft is an AI tool for professional designers, illustrators, and marketers, setting a new standard for excellence in image generation.

We designed a tool that lets creators quickly generate and iterate original images, vector art, illustrations, icons, and 3D graphics with AI. Over 3 million users across 190+ countries have produced hundreds of millions of images using Recraft, and we're just getting started.

Join a universe of professional opportunities, develop and support large-scale projects, and shape the future of creativity. We are committed to making Recraft an essential, daily tool for every designer and setting the industry standard. Our mission is to ensure that creators can fully control their creative process with AI, providing them with innovative tools to turn ideas into reality.

If you’re passionate about pushing the boundaries of AI, we want you on board!




JOB DESCRIPTION



We are looking for experienced Research Engineers to develop and optimise AI models, conduct cutting-edge research, and push the boundaries of AI and machine learning. Whether your expertise lies in AI model development, applied research, or production-level deployment, this role offers the opportunity to work at the forefront of generative AI innovation.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES



 - Develop and train large-scale generative models, pushing the boundaries of AI capabilities.

 - Design and run experiments to explore and refine model architectures, analyze results, and implement data-driven improvements.

 - Continuously innovate by developing and deploying new features that enhance user experiences.

‍


REQUIREMENTS



 - Proven experience in training large-scale generative models (e.g., image generation, audio synthesis, or large language models).

 - Expertise in evaluating generative models, with a deep understanding of qualitative and quantitative metrics.

 - Strong proficiency in PyTorch and familiarity with state-of-the-art neural network architectures.

 - Hands-on experience building end-to-end machine learning pipelines, including data collection and pre-processing, model training and evaluation, inference serving.

 - Strong software engineering skills, ability to design, implement and maintain high-quality code.

 - English: B2+ (Upper-Intermediate or above), written and spoken.

‍


WHAT WE OFFER



 - Competitive salary and equity.

 - Opportunities for professional growth and leadership.

 - A collaborative, research-driven environment.

 - The chance to work on cutting-edge AI projects that redefine creative workflows.

 - The opportunity to contribute to open-source AI research and publications.

 - Full-time, on-site role.

 - We’re able to offer Skilled Worker visa sponsorship in the UK for qualified candidates.

‍

source_jobs.title: ML Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Founded in the US in 2022 and now based in London, UK, Recraft is an AI tool for professional designers, illustrators, and marketers, setting a new standard for excellence in image generation.

We designed a tool that lets creators quickly generate and iterate original images, vector art, illustrations, icons, and 3D graphics with AI. Over 3 million users across 190+ countries have produced hundreds of millions of images using Recraft, and we're just getting started.

Join a universe of professional opportunities, develop and support large-scale projects, and shape the future of creativity. We are committed to making Recraft an essential, daily tool for every designer and setting the industry standard. Our mission is to ensure that creators can fully control their creative process with AI, providing them with innovative tools to turn ideas into reality.

If you’re passionate about pushing the boundaries of AI, we want you on board!


JOB DESCRIPTION

At Recraft, we’re building the next generation of generative models across images and text. We’re looking for an ML Data Engineer to scale our data pipelines for unstructured data (primarily images) and keep our training flows fast, reliable, and repeatable. You’ll design and operate high-throughput ingestion and preprocessing on Kubernetes, evolve our internal data-pipeline framework, and work hand-in-hand with ML engineers to ship datasets that move model quality forward.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Develop and maintain data-ingestion pipelines to source and prepare large-scale image (and occasional text/HTML) datasets from open, publicly accessible, and permitted sources.

 - Own the end-to-end flow: raw data → quality/beauty/relevance filtering → dedup/validation → ready-to-train artifacts.
   Operate and improve our Kubernetes-based data-pipeline framework (distributed jobs, retries, monitoring, automation).

 - Work with S3-style object storage: efficient layouts, lifecycle, throughput, and cost awareness.

 - Add tooling around pipelines (progress/health visualization, metrics, alerts) for observability and faster iteration.

 - Collaborate closely with ML engineers to align datasets with training needs and accelerate experimentation.
   


REQUIREMENTS

Must-have

 - Strong Python fundamentals; you write clean, maintainable, production-ready code.

 - Solid hands-on Kubernetes experience (containers, jobs, batch/distributed processing).

 - Proven track record with unstructured data, especially images (loading, filtering, transforming at scale).

 - Experience developing data-ingestion or parsing tools for publicly accessible sources, including handling real-world reliability and failure cases gracefully.

 - Comfort with S3/object storage and moving lots of data efficiently and safely.

 - Pragmatic, detail-oriented, ownership mindset; you enjoy making systems reliable and fast.

 - English: B2+ (Upper-Intermediate or above), written and spoken.
   

Nice-to-have

 - Familiarity with ML workflows (PyTorch) and downstream training considerations.

 - Experience with image quality scoring, captioning, or image-to-text pipelines.

 - DAG/workflow visualizations or pipeline UX tooling.

 - DevOps fluency: Docker, CI/CD, infra automation.
   


WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive salary and equity.

 - We’re able to offer Skilled Worker visa sponsorship in the UK for qualified candidates.

 - Real impact on model quality: your pipelines directly power training runs and product improvements.

 - Ownership with support: autonomy to design and improve systems, alongside experienced ML peers.

 - Modern stack: Python, Kubernetes, S3, internal pipeline framework built for scale.

 - Growth: a fast-moving environment where shipping well-engineered systems is the norm.

 - Full-time, on-site role.

source_jobs.title: AI Designer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Founded in the US in 2022 and now based in London, UK, Recraft is an AI tool for professional designers, illustrators, and marketers, setting a new standard for excellence in image generation.

We designed a tool that lets creators quickly generate and iterate original images, vector art, illustrations, icons, and 3D graphics with AI. Over 3 million users across 190+ countries have produced hundreds of millions of images using Recraft, and we're just getting started.

Join a universe of professional opportunities, develop and support large-scale projects, and shape the future of creativity. We are committed to making Recraft an essential, daily tool for every designer and setting the industry standard. Our mission is to ensure that creators can fully control their creative process with AI, providing them with innovative tools to turn ideas into reality.

If you’re passionate about pushing the boundaries of AI, we want you on board!


ABOUT THE ROLE

You’ll work at the intersection of design and machine learning, applying our model to real creative scenarios to uncover where it works, where it fails, and how it can improve.

We’re looking for practicing designers who work from real briefs, references, and constraints — not artificial prompt experiments.

Your taste, judgment, and ability to define quality will directly shape how the model evolves.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Apply generative models to real-world design scenarios, including editorial illustration, marketing visuals, product imagery, brand assets, and early-stage concept ideation

 - Evaluate generated outputs to identify quality gaps, edge cases, and opportunities for improvement, documenting findings clearly and constructively

 - Compare and rank generated images using defined criteria such as composition, visual quality, consistency, and usability within product or marketing contexts

 - Source and curate image sets from permitted and licensed sources that reflect contemporary visual trends and broadly recognized style categories

 - Develop realistic use-case scenarios with clear objectives, constraints, and desired outcomes to guide model evaluation and iteration




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3+ years of professional experience as a designer, illustrator, art director, or visual creative

 - A portfolio with recent and relevant work

 - Strong visual taste and aesthetic judgment

 - Experience working with real design briefs and constraints

 - Ability to clearly explain why one visual solution is better than another

 - Comfort using experimental tools and providing structured feedback

 - Hands-on experience with AI image tools (and ideally chat-based AI)




NICE TO HAVE

 - Background in branding, marketing, editorial, product design, or illustration

 - Agency or studio experience across multiple brands

 - Freelance experience across diverse projects

 - Interest in AI, generative models, or creative tooling




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Full-time contract with flexible hours around core team availability

 - A front-row seat at the intersection of professional design and cutting-edge AI — you'll shape how the model evolves, not just test it

 - Direct impact: your taste and judgment will influence a product used by 3M+ designers worldwide

 - A small, focused team that moves fast and takes craft seriously

 - Competitive rate

source_jobs.title: Community Manager (X/Twitter & Discord)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Founded in the US in 2022 and now based in London, UK, Recraft is an AI tool for professional designers, illustrators, and marketers, setting a new standard for excellence in image generation.

We designed a tool that lets creators quickly generate and iterate original images, vector art, illustrations, icons, and 3D graphics with AI. Over 3 million users across 190+ countries have produced hundreds of millions of images using Recraft, and we're just getting started.

Join a universe of professional opportunities, develop and support large-scale projects, and shape the future of creativity. We are committed to making Recraft an essential, daily tool for every designer and setting the industry standard. Our mission is to ensure that creators can fully control their creative process with AI, providing them with innovative tools to turn ideas into reality.

If you’re passionate about pushing the boundaries of AI, we want you on board!


ABOUT THE ROLE

Recraft's community keeps showing us the same thing: a lot of creators are already talking about the product on their own, simply because they like it. We're building this role around nurturing that — someone who finds these creators, builds real relationships with them, and helps grow an ambassador community across Twitter and Discord, with Reddit on the roadmap. This isn't a classic outreach or support role: it blends community building, relationship management, and a genuine interest in the creator economy.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Identify and build relationships with active creators on Twitter (X) who already talk about Recraft, or who would genuinely enjoy using it

 - Onboard new ambassadors, explain how the program works, and support them as they get started

 - Maintain an organized, up-to-date pipeline of ambassadors — who they are, what they're working on, and how we can support them

 - Own the Discord community and experiment with new formats to keep it active

 - Share feedback with the team on what's working, what creators are saying, and where the program should evolve




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Genuine interest in social media, creators, and online communities — a strong plus if you run something similar yourself

 - Comfortable approaching problems without a fixed playbook; open-ended questions don't throw you off

 - Organized enough to track relationships with a growing number of people without anything slipping through the cracks

 - A real interest in talking to people and building relationships, not just posting content

 - Self-starter, comfortable working independently

 - Strong written English




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience in community management, creator/influencer outreach, or growth roles

 - Familiarity with ambassador or affiliate programs

 - Experience with CRM or outreach-tracking tools

 - An existing personal presence on Twitter or similar platforms




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Full-time, fully remote role

 - Close collaboration with the marketing team, with real input into how our community and brand voice evolve

 - A program built on genuine support for creators rather than rigid KPIs or referral fees

 - Competitive compensation.

source_jobs.title: Senior / Staff Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Founded in the US in 2022 and now based in London, UK, Recraft is an AI tool for professional designers, illustrators, and marketers, setting a new standard for excellence in image generation.

We designed a tool that lets creators quickly generate and iterate original images, vector art, illustrations, icons, and 3D graphics with AI. Over 3 million users across 190+ countries have produced hundreds of millions of images using Recraft, and we're just getting started.

Join a universe of professional opportunities, develop and support large-scale projects, and shape the future of creativity. We are committed to making Recraft an essential, daily tool for every designer and setting the industry standard. Our mission is to ensure that creators can fully control their creative process with AI, providing them with innovative tools to turn ideas into reality.

If you’re passionate about pushing the boundaries of AI, we want you on board!


ABOUT THE ROLE

Recraft builds a professional-grade design tool used by designers and creative teams around the world. We're looking for a Senior or Staff Product Designer to independently own key areas of the product, raise the bar on visual and interaction design, and work hand-in-hand with engineers from early concept through to shipped features. This is a hands-on, high-craft role for someone who enjoys designing at a granular level — the kind of detail that makes a professional tool feel effortless for power users.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Independently drive design projects end to end — from research and concept through to launch and iteration.

 - Design interfaces for a professional creative tool with a high level of detail, considering the nuances that matter most to power users.

 - Work closely with engineers throughout the process, including prototyping ideas quickly using code and AI-assisted tools (“vibe-coding”).

 - Present and advocate for your design decisions clearly and persuasively, across design, engineering and leadership.

 - Stay adaptable in a fast-moving environment where the product — and sometimes your own recent work — can change quickly based on what we learn.
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Substantial experience in product design at a senior or staff level, with a track record of independently owning and driving projects.

 - An exceptional eye for visual design and craft, with genuine attention to detail.

 - Strong, balanced skills across both UX and UI — equally comfortable with structure and polish.

 - Comfort working at the intersection of design and engineering, including prototyping with code or AI tools regardless of your current skill level.

 - Excellent communication skills and the ability to explain and sell your design thinking to different audiences.
   
   


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience designing professional tools or software for power users.

 - A native or near-native English speaker, adding linguistic and cultural diversity to the design team.
   
   


WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive compensation in line with market rates for senior/staff-level talent.

 - A structured performance review process to support your development and career growth.Performance-based bonuses tied to your impact and the company's results.

 - The opportunity to shape a category-defining product and set the design bar for a fast-growing team.

 - A high level of ownership and autonomy over your area of the product.

 - Close collaboration with founders and leadership — your work and voice will carry real weight.

 - A flexible, hybrid-friendly setup within the UK.

 - A high-craft, low-bureaucracy culture where good design decisions move fast.

 - We’re able to offer Skilled Worker visa sponsorship in the UK for qualified candidates.

notes: AI-focused product with strong engineering core, recent hiring activity
